infobox.value: | | | | [Kingsgrove](/wiki/Kingsgrove,_New_South_Wales "Kingsgrove, New South Wales") | [Clemton Park](/wiki/Clemton_Park,_New_South_Wales "Clemton Park, New South Wales") | [Earlwood](/wiki/Earlwood,_New_South_Wales "Earlwood, New South Wales") | | [Kingsgrove](/wiki/Kingsgrove,_New_South_Wales "Kingsgrove, New South Wales") | Bexley North | [Bardwell Park](/wiki/Enfield,_New_South_Wales "Enfield, New South Wales") | | [Bexley](/wiki/Bexley,_New_South_Wales "Bexley, New South Wales") | [Bexley](/wiki/Bexley,_New_South_Wales "Bexley, New South Wales") | [Bexley](/wiki/Bexley,_New_South_Wales "Bexley, New South Wales") | | |

is a [suburb](/wiki/Suburb "Suburb") in [southern Sydney](/wiki/Southern_Sydney "Southern Sydney"), in the state of [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), 13 kilometres (8.1 miles) south-west of the [Sydney CBD](/wiki/Sydney_central_business_district "Sydney central business district"). It is part of the [St George](/wiki/St_George,_New_South_Wales "St George, New South Wales") area. Bexley North is in the [local government area](/wiki/Local_government_in_Australia "Local government in Australia") of the [Bayside Council](/wiki/Bayside_Council "Bayside Council").

[Australian Bureau of Statistics](/wiki/Australian_Bureau_of_Statistics "Australian Bureau of Statistics") (28 June 2022). ["Bexley North (suburb and locality)"](https://www.abs.gov.au/census/find-census-data/quickstats/2021/SAL10334). *Australian Census 2021 QuickStats*. [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f9/Bexley_North.jpg/250px-Bexley_North.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bexley_North.jpg)

Corner of Bexley Road and Slade Road

**Bexley North** is a [suburb](/wiki/Suburb "Suburb") in [southern Sydney](/wiki/Southern_Sydney "Southern Sydney"), in the state of [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), 13 kilometres (8.1 miles) south-west of the [Sydney CBD](/wiki/Sydney_central_business_district "Sydney central business district"). It is part of the [St George](/wiki/St_George,_New_South_Wales "St George, New South Wales") area. Bexley North is in the [local government area](/wiki/Local_government_in_Australia "Local government in Australia") of the [Bayside Council](/wiki/Bayside_Council "Bayside Council").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bexley_North&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [[icon]](/wiki/File:Wiki_letter_w_cropped.svg) | This section **needs expansion**. You can help by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Bexley_North&action=edit&section=). *(July 2017)* |

James Chandler named [Bexley](/wiki/Bexley "Bexley") after his birthplace in [London](/wiki/London "London") (formerly [Kent](/wiki/Kent "Kent")), [England](/wiki/England "England"). Chandler bought Sylvester's Farm in 1822, from Thomas Sylvester who had been granted the land about ten years earlier. That year he was also granted 1,200 acres (4.9 km2) of land which stretched from what is now Bexley North to most of [Rockdale](/wiki/Rockdale,_New_South_Wales "Rockdale, New South Wales") and [Kogarah](/wiki/Kogarah,_New_South_Wales "Kogarah, New South Wales"). Development in the area began in 1884 with the railway line to [Hurstville](/wiki/Hurstville,_New_South_Wales "Hurstville, New South Wales").

The opening of the East Hills line and the railway station at Bexley North in 1931, opened up the area for home sites.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Commercial area

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bexley_North&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Commercial area")]

Bexley North is mostly a residential suburb with a small shopping centre located around the intersection of Bexley Road with Slade Road and Shaw Street, close to Bexley North railway station. It features a [Woolworths Metro supermarket](/wiki/Woolworths_Supermarkets "Woolworths Supermarkets"), a pub, specialty shops including a prominently placed funeral parlour, cafes and restaurants.

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bexley_North&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Transport")]

[Bexley North railway station](/wiki/Bexley_North_railway_station "Bexley North railway station") is on the [Airport & South Line](/wiki/Airport_%26_South_Line "Airport & South Line") of the [Sydney Trains](/wiki/Sydney_Trains "Sydney Trains") network. Bexley North is also serviced by [Transit Systems](/wiki/Transit_Systems_NSW "Transit Systems NSW") bus routes and private bus routes.

The [M5 South Western Motorway](/wiki/M5_South_Western_Motorway "M5 South Western Motorway") runs south-west towards [Beverly Hills](/wiki/Beverly_Hills,_New_South_Wales "Beverly Hills, New South Wales") and [Liverpool](/wiki/Liverpool,_New_South_Wales "Liverpool, New South Wales"). There are no entrances at Bexley North to the 4 km tunnel which begins here and heads north-east towards [Botany](/wiki/Botany,_New_South_Wales "Botany, New South Wales") and the city. Access to the north east of the motorway is allowed at [Kingsgrove](/wiki/Kingsgrove,_New_South_Wales "Kingsgrove, New South Wales") and [Arncliffe](/wiki/Arncliffe,_New_South_Wales "Arncliffe, New South Wales").

## Landmarks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bexley_North&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Landmarks")]

* Bardwell Creek and the Prime Minister's Walk to Bexley Pool
* Bexley North Hotel
* Bexley North Library
* Bexley North Public School
* Bexley North Scout Hall
* Holy Trinity Anglican Church
* Stotts Reserve

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bexley_North&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Demographics")]

According to the [2021 census](/wiki/2021_Australian_census "2021 Australian census") of population, there were 4,281 people usually resident in Bexley North. 57.7% of people were born in Australia. The next most common countries of birth were China 6.6% and Greece 5.5%. 43.0% of people only spoke English at home. Other languages spoken at home included Greek 16.0%, Cantonese 6.4%, Arabic 6.0% and Mandarin 5.9%. The most common responses for religion were Eastern Orthodox 23.5%, No Religion 23.3% and Catholic 21.1%.[[2]](#cite_note-abs2021-2)

## Notable residents

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bexley_North&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Notable residents")]

* [Tim Cahill](/wiki/Tim_Cahill "Tim Cahill"), Australian footballer, playing for [the Socceroos](/wiki/Australia_men%27s_national_soccer_team "Australia men's national soccer team") and [Everton](/wiki/Everton_F.C. "Everton F.C.") was a student at Bexley North Primary School.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
* [Anthony Totten](/wiki/Anthony_Totten "Anthony Totten"), former Professional Rugby League footballer, grew up in the suburb.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* [Bob Windle](/wiki/Bob_Windle "Bob Windle"), gold medalist in the Men's 1500m Swimming Freestyle at the [1964 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1964_Summer_Olympics "1964 Summer Olympics"), grew up in the suburb.
